title: centaurea iberica subsp. meryonis
intro: Centaurea iberica subsp. meryonis, also known as Meryon's knapweed or Meryon's centaury, is a plant species in the dandelion family, native to the Caucasus region and Turkey. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Centaurea iberica subsp. meryonis include reducing inflammation and pain, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The plant is traditionally prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems.
